outcome.note: Pending until the committed schema transition is trained in the next iteration.

lesson: Poor landing quality due to weak centering, slow descent, and no speed penalty leads to bouncing and off-center landings. Edits strengthen these aspects while preserving safety.

reports.reason: No candidate feasibility check for this structural edit type.

reflection_strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Improving soft landing requires simultaneously strengthening centering, increasing descent incentive, and adding a continuous speed penalty. Changing one without the others risks drift: e.g., stronger descent without lateral control could lead to off-centre touchdowns; a speed penalty without centering might not suffice. The package must be applied together to maintain balanced behaviour.
editor_constraints: Increase r_center_guidance weight modestly (e.g., from 0.5 to 0.7–0.9) to improve lateral accuracy without re-creating dominance (current ratio 0.106)
Increase r_descent_guidance weight or modify its formula to also reward faster descent (e.g., 1 - y) but ensure it does not overwhelm other signals; current weight 0.5, could be raised to 0.8–1.0
Introduce a new dense component or modify existing one to penalise high speed (vx and vy) throughout the episode, with a clip [–1,0], to encourage gentle descent and discourage bouncing
Optionally slightly increase r_fuel_penalty weight (currently 1.0) to improve fuel efficiency
Keep all safety constraints and terminal success event unchanged
Avoid making terminal success the sole strong reward; maintain dense progress shaping
must_preserve: stability component (r_stability) to prevent excessive tilt
terminal landing success event (r_landing_success) as goal signal
existing action mapping and variable set
must_avoid: increasing any component weight to a dominant ratio (>0.8)
relying solely on sparse terminal reward
introducing non-observable variables or infinite reward loops
reflection_strategy.expected_effect: Agent should learn to descend more quickly and softly, reduce bouncing (contact toggles should drop), and centre more accurately. Centering reward should increase towards 0.4–0.5, descent guidance mean should rise, and landing events should become more stable. Task score and semantic score should improve further.
reflection_strategy.risk_analysis: If centering weight is increased too much, it may dominate again and discourage descent. If speed penalty is too strong, it may cause overly cautious, slow approach. If descent incentive is boosted too aggressively, the agent might crash. The package needs careful scaling. Monitor crash rate and contact toggles after training.

reflection_report.reflection_summary: The agent now consistently achieves ground contact and receives the terminal landing reward, but landing quality is poor: high contact toggling (bouncing), weak centering (r_center_guidance mean 0.13), slow average descent (r_descent_guidance mean 0.305), and moderate fuel use. This is a shaping-goal mismatch: the dense components do not adequately incentivise a soft, centred, fuel-efficient landing, leading to unstable contact behaviour. The previous edit successfully introduced descent incentive, but further refinement is needed to improve landing smoothness and precision.

observed_facts: All 6 episodes triggered r_landing_success (terminal reward paid rate 1.0) but success episode rate is 0.0 because landings are not stable/safe
High contact toggling: mean 106.8, max 140, indicating bouncing or intermittent ground contact
r_center_guidance mean 0.13, max 0.498 – lateral positioning is suboptimal
r_descent_guidance mean 0.305 (1 - y), implying average y ≈ 0.695 – descent is not aggressive
r_stability mean -0.074 – moderate tilt/angular velocity
r_fuel_penalty mean -0.03 – fuel cost is moderate
No crashes detected
Hack score 0.2 with shaping_goal_mismatch flag true, no reward hacking
likely_true_failures: Insufficient incentive for soft, slow touchdown – r_vertical_safety only activates when y < 0.1 and penalises |vy|, but does not encourage gentle descent overall
Weak lateral centering – r_center_guidance weight too low to adequately pull the lander to x=0
Descent speed is not penalised – agent can descend quickly and bounce, as there is no cost for high vx or vy away from ground
Fuel efficiency could be improved by slightly increasing fuel penalty
likely_false_positives: The terminal_success_dominance flag (component ratio 0.512) is benign – landing is the ultimate goal, and dense shaping is still present
Unstable contact toggling is not reward hacking; it is a task performance issue
root_cause_hypotheses: The dense reward components are not sufficiently aligned with the desired soft-landing behaviour: centering is too weak, descent incentive exists but does not penalise speed, and vertical safety only operates very close to ground
The r_vertical_safety condition is too restrictive (only y < 0.1) and the penalty is only on vy; a broader speed penalty or continuous vertical control feedback could stabilise touchdown
Fuel cost is mild and could be increased to discourage inefficient hovering/thrust

reusable_lessons: lesson_iter_000_to_001: Rebalancing package (reduce dominant component, tighten vertical safety condition, add descent guidance) successfully transitioned from hovering to landing; the general principle of coupled rebalancing and introducing missing shaping is reusable
Outcome lesson from iter_000-001: The edit improved task and semantic scores without introducing true reward hacking; dense progress shaping must accompany terminal rewards
avoid_actions: Removing or severely weakening centering guidance – some horizontal alignment is essential
Making terminal success the sole strong reward without intermediate dense shaping
Drastically increasing any single component weight to dominance (>0.8 ratio)
recommended_actions: Adjust weights of existing dense components to improve centering and descent speed, and add a continuous velocity penalty to dampen bouncing
Keep all safety constraints (crash, stability) intact
Maintain the coupled rebalancing approach: edits should be atomic to avoid new drift

memory_use_policy.raw_memory_reason: The single raw memory card (iter_000_to_001) provides a concrete before/after trace of a successful edit that addressed a similar dominance and shaping-gap issue; it is directly relevant for understanding how dense rebalancing works in this environment.
memory_use_policy.lesson_memory_reason: Distilled lesson from that memory captures effective strategies (reduce dominant weight, introduce missing shaping, tighten safety conditions) and warns against single-component dominance; it guides the next edit.
memory_use_policy.outcome_lesson_reason: The outcome lesson confirms the edit was accepted with positive task/semantic deltas and no hack risk; it supports the decision to continue refining via similar coupled edits.

strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Improving soft landing requires simultaneously strengthening centering, increasing descent incentive, and adding a continuous speed penalty. Changing one without the others risks drift: e.g., stronger descent without lateral control could lead to off-centre touchdowns; a speed penalty without centering might not suffice. The package must be applied together to maintain balanced behaviour.
editor_constraints: Increase r_center_guidance weight modestly (e.g., from 0.5 to 0.7–0.9) to improve lateral accuracy without re-creating dominance (current ratio 0.106)
Increase r_descent_guidance weight or modify its formula to also reward faster descent (e.g., 1 - y) but ensure it does not overwhelm other signals; current weight 0.5, could be raised to 0.8–1.0
Introduce a new dense component or modify existing one to penalise high speed (vx and vy) throughout the episode, with a clip [–1,0], to encourage gentle descent and discourage bouncing
Optionally slightly increase r_fuel_penalty weight (currently 1.0) to improve fuel efficiency
Keep all safety constraints and terminal success event unchanged
Avoid making terminal success the sole strong reward; maintain dense progress shaping
must_preserve: stability component (r_stability) to prevent excessive tilt
terminal landing success event (r_landing_success) as goal signal
existing action mapping and variable set
must_avoid: increasing any component weight to a dominant ratio (>0.8)
relying solely on sparse terminal reward
introducing non-observable variables or infinite reward loops
strategy.expected_effect: Agent should learn to descend more quickly and softly, reduce bouncing (contact toggles should drop), and centre more accurately. Centering reward should increase towards 0.4–0.5, descent guidance mean should rise, and landing events should become more stable. Task score and semantic score should improve further.
strategy.risk_analysis: If centering weight is increased too much, it may dominate again and discourage descent. If speed penalty is too strong, it may cause overly cautious, slow approach. If descent incentive is boosted too aggressively, the agent might crash. The package needs careful scaling. Monitor crash rate and contact toggles after training.

package_should_be_rejected_if: r_center_guidance weight is increased above 1.0 or its ratio exceeds 0.5
no measure is added to reduce high-speed descent (e.g., no vy/vx penalty)
any safety constraint (crash, stability) is weakened or removed
terminal success weight is increased without corresponding dense shaping
a new component dominates the reward (ratio >0.8)
package_should_be_accepted_if: centering guidance weight is moderately increased, aiming for r_center_guidance ratio ~0.2–0.3
a continuous speed penalty (e.g., -k*(|vx|+|vy|)) is introduced with clip [–1,0], weight 0.3–0.5
descent guidance weight is increased or its scaling adjusted to yield faster descent average
all safety and cost constraints remain intact
the edit package maintains overall reward balance without introducing new dominance
safety_notes: Ensure the new speed penalty does not penalise necessary descent velocity too harshly; consider only penalising above a threshold or using a soft function (e.g., tanh)
Monitor contact toggles; if they remain high, additional ground-contact smoothing may be needed (e.g., bonus for both contacts staying true without toggling)
If descent becomes too rapid and crashes appear, the package should be rolled back or balanced with stronger vertical safety near ground

outcome_decision.reason: Internal task/semantic evidence improved enough to become the current best candidate without true semantic hack risk.

schema_transition.reason: Final edit plan committed to next schema.

lesson_assessments.reason: Previous edit successfully reduced center dominance and added descent guidance, proving that coupled rebalancing can improve task outcomes without hacking.

reusable_lessons: Decrease dominant component weight and introduce missing dense progress shaping
Coupled edits (weight adjustments + new shaping) are more reliable than single changes
avoid_actions: Removing or severely weakening centering guidance
Making terminal success the sole strong reward without intermediate dense shaping
Drastically increasing any single component weight to dominance (>0.8 ratio)
recommended_actions: Increase weight of r_center_guidance and r_descent_guidance moderately
Add a continuous speed penalty (vx and vy) to dampen bouncing

reward_editor.rationale: To improve landing quality, strengthen centering and descent incentives, and add a continuous speed penalty to reduce bouncing, while keeping safety constraints intact. Previous memory supports this coupled approach.
reward_editor.expected_effect: Agent should land more centered, descend faster, and with lower speeds; contact toggling should decrease; success rate may remain high but landing stability should improve.
reward_editor.risk_analysis: If centering or descent weights are too high, they could overshadow other objectives; speed penalty might discourage necessary descent velocity; but moderate increases (1.5×) and a mild penalty weight (0.3) limit this risk. Monitoring after training will detect any new dominance or crash increase.

warnings: New dense component may contribute 1.50x terminal scale; reduce weight or run candidate sweep.

what_worked: Moderate weight increases on centering and descent components
Adding a continuous speed penalty with clip [-1,0]
avoid_next: Overweighting any single dense component to dominance
recommend_next: Monitor contact toggles and adjust speed penalty scaling if needed
Consider further incremental weight tuning if landing remains unstable
applicability_notes: Applicable when terminal reward is achieved but landing quality is poor (bouncing, weak centering, slow descent).
